Description: A purchasing card (physical or virtual) issued to a Cardholder. Tracks card type (physical/virtual) credit limit processor reference and lifecycle status.
Notes: PCard domain entirely absent from inventory

Description: Registry record for an employee enrolled in the card program. Tracks default coding preferences approver assignment and lifecycle status independent of individual card issuance.
Notes: PCard domain entirely absent from inventory

Description: Configuration record for a purchasing card program at an entity. Defines credit limits approval routing statement cycle dates GL clearing account and bank vendor link.
Notes: PCard domain entirely absent from inventory

Description: A bank-feed transaction imported from the card processor. Tracks merchant details amount and lifecycle through coding approval and GL posting.
Notes: PCard domain entirely absent from inventory

Description: Per-card billing statement grouping a period's transactions. Lifecycle: OPEN → SUBMITTED → APPROVED/REJECTED → INVOICED → PAID. GL posting is statement-level and all-or-nothing.
Notes: PCard domain entirely absent from inventory

Description: Record of a bank-feed file upload. Tracks parse status row count duplicate skips and error details.
Notes: PCard domain entirely absent from inventory

Description: A contact person associated with a vendor capturing communication details roles and status used to coordinate vendor relationships.
A compliance certificate for a vendor including type issue date and expiry. Tracks qualifications compliance or insurance requirements (e.g. DBE minority-owned).
A file attachment associated with a vendor record for compliance documentation or contract purposes.
A vendor address for billing remit-to or shipping purposes used in procurement workflows.
A vendor payment method with banking details (ACH wire check) and default preferences for disbursement.
IRS 1099 tax information for a vendor used for year-end tax reporting and compliance.
An entity-specific vendor profile capturing local government-specific data for a vendor.
A cross-suite work item scoped to an entity with optional assignee subject URN actions and outcome tracking. Intentionally distinct from Labor Task (EAM-specific) per ADR-65.
Notes: Different from Labor Task — ADR-65 calls this out explicitly

Description: A utility service definition (water sewer electricity gas) that can be assigned to a customer Account at a specific service location.
Notes: Sub-entity of RevenueCore; Service in Bifrost

Description: A versioned schedule of rate tiers defining billing rates for a utility service. Rate Codes reference Rate Schedules for charge calculation.
A GL posting rule linking charge types to the appropriate general ledger account to ensure billed charges post to the correct revenue account.
A link record associating a specific Utility Service with a customer Account including start date and end date.
A mailing address on a utility customer record used for billing statements and correspondence.
A phone or contact record on a utility customer record.
A dimension in the Chart of Accounts structure (Fund Department Object Program Project). Defines how financial data is classified and reported. Present in sheet 2 but not sheet 1.
Notes: In gsheet sheet 2 definition tab only

Description: A hierarchical grouping of segment codes within a CoA segment used for reporting hierarchies and budget roll-ups.
A fiscal year containing open or closed fiscal periods. Controls which periods are available for journal entry posting and period-end close processes.
Configuration mapping fund segment codes to their associated segment IDs and controlling fund-based account behavior and subledger integration.
Notes: Technical config — debatable as domain object

Description: Mapping between fund segment codes and COA accounts defining which accounts are valid for each fund for cross-suite posting.
Notes: Technical config — debatable as domain object

Description: An export job record managing data export operations from the General Ledger allowing consumers to trigger exports and retrieve results.
Notes: Operational entity — debatable as domain object

Description: A record confirming goods or services received against a Purchase Order. Required for three-way match (PO + Receipt + Invoice). Listed in gsheet sheet 2 but absent from primary sheet 1 inventory.
Notes: In gsheet sheet 2 only — should be in sheet 1
